title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 potential layoffs if a proposed property-tax override fails, officials warn.
The district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain current staffing and programs.
Brookline voters to decide on the proposed override during the May 5 annual town election.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to regulate pet sales and housing, reducing reliance on puppy mills in Massachusetts.
The bill would allow pet shops to partner with animal shelters for adoptions, banning direct sales of pets.
The legislation also seeks to remove housing and insurance barriers, promoting accessible pet ownership across the state.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts officials urge water conservation due to severe drought persisting despite heavy winter snowfall.
Central and Northeast regions elevated to Level 3 Critical Drought; Connecticut River Valley at Level 2 Significant Drought.
Massachusetts Water Resources Authority system isn't experiencing drought, but officials recommend conservation to residents.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph discusses why some straight women are choosing not to feature boyfriends prominently on social media.
Joseph's viral essay sparks debate about traditional relationship models and the independence of women in heterosexual partnerships.
The Love Letters podcast explores Joseph's insights, examining societal expectations and the role of romantic relationships in women's lives.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill, 27, is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides medically safe recreational experiences for children with serious medical conditions free of charge.
O'Neill's fiancee's sister, Reagan Carey, a former camper, now serves as a camp counselor after cancer remission.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles will not trade wide receiver A.J. Brown to the Patriots at this time, per reports.
Trade discussions may resume closer to June 1 when the Eagles could benefit from salary cap relief.
The Patriots reportedly offered a first- and third-round pick for Brown, which the Eagles denied.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum set to return against the Washington Wizards after being left off Boston's injury report.
Tatum, recovering from a ruptured Achilles, is on a minutes restriction and missed Thursday's game for injury management.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out for the Celtics' Saturday game.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's 'War Machine', starring Alan Ritchson, explores a soldier's PTSD and is now available for streaming.
'Zootopia 2' follows Nick and Judy's mystery-solving adventures and is streaming on Disney+.
HBO Max's 'Rooster' stars Steve Carell as an author finding new beginnings on a college campus.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots add former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker to strengthen offensive line after struggling with 68 sacks allowed last season.
Vera-Tucker missed the entire previous season due to injuries but has started 43 NFL games, mostly at right guard.
Morgan Moses, former teammate at the Jets, influenced Vera-Tucker's decision to join Patriots, citing a strong team culture.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora identified Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as key leaders for the 2026 Boston Red Sox.
Trevor Story led the Red Sox in home runs, RBIs, runs, and hits during the 2025 season.
Garrett Crochet was recognized for his consistency, leading MLB in strikeouts with 255 in 2025.
